data.title: Mutual Funds Face Challenges Despite Strong Performance
data.description:
Eric Balchunas, senior ETF Analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, posted on X that mutual funds, despite being potentially suitable for private company exposure, are experiencing outflows. This trend persists even as these funds perform exceptionally well. In contrast, the $RONB fund has attracted $90 million in just one month, highlighting a shift in investor preference.

Balchunas also noted the growing interest in thematically appropriate ETFs incorporating private company exposure as part of their strategy. He predicts that within two years, numerous ETFs will adopt this approach, reflecting a significant trend in the investment landscape. The mention of $XOVR and $AGIX further underscores the evolving dynamics in the market.

data.title: European Stock Index Futures Decline
data.description: European stock index futures experienced a downturn, with the Euro Stoxx 50 and Germany's DAX index futures both falling by 0.4%. According to ChainCatcher, this decline reflects broader market trends impacting major European indices.

data.title: CFTC Chairman Selig Advocates for Stable Crypto Regulations
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump's newly appointed Chairman of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), Mike Selig, has announced plans to establish a regulatory framework for the cryptocurrency industry. According to ChainCatcher, Selig aims to create rules that will provide long-term protection for crypto assets and other financial innovations, ensuring they are not easily overturned in the future.

In an article published on social media platform X and in The Washington Post on Tuesday, Selig stated that the CFTC will move away from its current practice of enforcement-based regulation. Instead, it will develop tailored rules for emerging markets like digital assets to maintain the global competitiveness of U.S. financial markets. He noted that the digital asset economy has grown from a novelty to a market valued at approximately $3 trillion. While specific regulatory details have not yet been disclosed, Selig mentioned that more policy adjustments will be announced in the coming days. Additionally, an Innovation Advisory Committee will be established to provide guidance on financial innovations, including crypto assets, prediction markets, and perpetual contracts.

Selig emphasized that regulatory policies formed through formal rule-making processes are more stable and less likely to be overturned by future administrations compared to informal guidance. On the same day, the CFTC also announced the hiring of crypto attorney Michael Passalacqua to assist with related efforts. Analysts suggest that this stance aligns with the Trump administration's overall supportive position on crypto innovation, indicating that the CFTC may become one of the most crucial regulatory bodies for the U.S. crypto market in the future.

data.title: Europe's Significant Role in U.S. Debt Financing Amid Greenland Tensions
data.description: Europe possesses nearly $8 trillion in U.S. Treasuries, bonds, and equities, positioning it as a vital contributor to U.S. debt financing. According to NS3.AI, there are growing concerns that U.S. President Donald Trump's interest in Greenland could trigger a substantial sell-off by European investors. Such a move could destabilize U.S. assets and elevate political risk premiums. This scenario underscores the U.S.'s reliance on foreign investors to finance its deficits, potentially diminishing its influence in geopolitical matters such as the Greenland issue.

data.title: Gold Surges in 2025 Amid Tariff Uncertainty; Bitcoin and Oil Prices Decline
data.description: In 2025, gold experienced a significant increase of 62.6% as a result of uncertainty caused by tariffs. According to NS3.AI, oil prices fell by 21.5% due to decreased demand, while Bitcoin saw a decline of 6.4%. Despite Bitcoin's stagnation, Digital Asset Treasury Companies invested nearly $50 billion, substantially increasing their holdings and concentration of Bitcoin and Ethereum. This institutional accumulation, occurring amid liquidity constraints, indicates a strong conviction and sets the stage for a potential rebound in the crypto market in 2026.

data.title: Intel's Stock Surge and AI Partnership with Nvidia Fuel Optimism
data.description: Intel's stock has experienced a remarkable increase of over 130% in the past year, driven by new chip launches and a strategic partnership with Nvidia in artificial intelligence. According to NS3.AI, analysts are optimistic about continued growth, with several firms, including Seaport Research and HSBC, significantly raising Intel's price targets. This optimism is largely based on anticipated strong growth in server CPUs, fueled by rising AI demand. Intel's forthcoming Q4 2025 earnings report is eagerly awaited, as it may confirm these positive forecasts and further impact market sentiment.

data.title: Forecast market weekly fees exceeded $2.7 million, setting a new record.
data.description: According to Dune data, prediction market revenue exceeded $2.7 million in a single week, setting a new record. The opinion market accounted for 54.3% of this, while Polymarket's 15-minute price fluctuation market generated $787,000 in revenue, representing 28.4% of total revenue.

data.title: DeepSeek Unveils New Model 'MODEL1' on Anniversary of DeepSeek-R1
data.description: On January 21, DeepSeek marked the first anniversary of DeepSeek-R1 by revealing details about its new model, 'MODEL1.' According to BlockBeats, the company updated its FlashMLA code on GitHub, highlighting 28 mentions of MODEL1 across 114 files. This model appears alongside V32, indicating it is distinct from DeepSeek-V3.2. The differences in the code are evident in areas such as KV cache layout, sparsity handling, and FP8 decoding, with several optimizations in memory management.

data.title: Bitcoin and Other Cryptocurrencies See Surge in Social Media Discussions
data.description: Santiment posted on X that recent social media metrics indicate a significant rise in discussion rates for several cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin (BTC). This trend highlights growing interest and engagement within the crypto community. The increase in conversations around these digital assets suggests heightened attention from investors and enthusiasts alike. As discussions continue to rise, market participants are closely monitoring the potential impacts on cryptocurrency prices and market dynamics.

data.title: Discussion on Profits in the Market
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X about hearing reports of significant profits being made by individuals in the market. The conversation highlights the ongoing interest and activity within the financial sector, where many are reportedly achieving substantial gains. This reflects the dynamic nature of the market and the opportunities it presents for investors.

data.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary Predicts 2026 GDP Growth Above 5%
data.description:
U. S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick forecasts over 5% GDP growth in Q1 2026, signaling economic optimism.
source: https://coincu.com/news/us-commerce-secretary-gdp-2026/

data.title: Institutions predict gold prices may hit $5,000 in the first half of the year.
data.description: With escalating global macroeconomic uncertainty, the market trends of precious and base metals are attracting significant attention. Joni Teves, a precious metals strategist at UBS, recently stated in an interview that the demand for diversified asset allocation is the core driver of the current gold price increase, with institutional investors, retail investors, and central banks all increasing their gold holdings to cope with macroeconomic uncertainties. She predicts that gold prices will continue to rise in the first half of the year, and if market concerns about the independence of the Federal Reserve continue to intensify, gold prices could potentially challenge the $5,000/ounce mark in the first half of the year. Silver, benefiting from rising gold prices and its own narrowing supply-demand gap, may challenge $100/ounce this year. The copper market, driven by energy transition demand, is experiencing a tightening supply-demand situation, and its price center is expected to rise. (Golden Ten)

data.title: CFTC Chairman: Congress is about to pass the Digital Asset Market Transparency Act.
data.description: The chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) stated that Congress is close to passing the Digital Asset Market Transparency Act. The chairman indicated that a tailored regulatory framework will be implemented to ensure the healthy development of these markets within the United States. (Bitcoin Magazine)

data.title: FTC goes for round two to break Meta’s monopoly
data.description:
The US Federal Trade Commission said it will challenge a judge’s decision from November that sided with Meta Platforms Inc. over its purchases of Instagram and WhatsApp. Judge James Boasberg said the deals for the photo-sharing app and messaging service didn’t break antitrust laws. He found the social networking company didn’t illegally control the market […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ftc-goes-for-round-two-to-break-metas-monopoly/

data.title: Wall Street’s secret blockchain platform is coming for your dividends and it’s using stablecoins to do it
data.description:
NYSE said it is developing a platform for trading and on-chain settlement of tokenized securities, and will seek regulatory approvals for a proposed new NYSE venue powered by that infrastructure. According to the owners, ICE, the system is designed to support 24/7 operations, instant settlement, orders sized in dollar amounts, and stablecoin-based funding.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/wall-streets-secret-blockchain-platform-is-coming-for-your-dividends-and-its-using-stablecoins-to-do-it/

data.title: U.S. GDP Growth Expected to Surpass 5% in Early 2026, Says Commerce Secretary
data.description:
U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick stated on January 21 that the country's GDP growth is anticipated to exceed 5% in the first quarter of 2026. According to BlockBeats, Lutnick highlighted that the current high interest rates are hindering stronger economic growth.

Speaking at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland, Lutnick remarked, "Our interest rates should be lower for the economy to truly thrive. I believe GDP will grow over 5% this quarter, which is significant for the $3 trillion U.S. economy. If rates were lower, growth could even reach 6%. Our current policies are holding us back."

It is important to note that Lutnick's growth forecast reflects his personal opinion and is higher than the expectations of U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant, who suggested at Davos that the real GDP growth in 2026 might range between 4% and 5%.

data.title: Huawei fights back as EU targets ‘high-risk’ suppliers
data.description:
Huawei has responded to the European Union’s plan to phase out its technology, calling the decision a violation of the union’s principles. The European Union had earlier proposed a new plan to remove suppliers that the bloc’s decision makers determine to be “high risk” from critical sectors. Huawei accuses the EU of violating its own […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/huawei-fights-eu-targets-high-risk-suppliers/
